parsed_review.summary: CP00-373 (Risk C, 150 units RP12.P02.M09.S09-RP12.P03.M06.S12) is a descriptor-only billing-core pack that closes RP12.P02 (M09 Claude Review Packet tail, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP12.P03 API foundation (M00-M06). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) of all in-scope implementation (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, test/model.test.js, contracts/billing-core-contract.json, scripts/validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs, README.md) plus pack evidence (manifest, command-evidence, baseline, readiness, validation-report, plan) confirms the pack-level claims hold. Scope/distributions are internally consistent across baseline/manifest/plan/contract/registry: I independently counted the 150 baseline units (implementation 63, contract 31, security_audit 16, test 20, claude_review 6, hermes_evidence 4, failure_recovery 4, ui 6 = 150), matching the manifest delivery_counts and BILLING_CORE_CP373_REQUIREMENTS exactly; first/last unit, upstream CP00-372, and next CP00-374 @ RP12.P03.M06.S13 all agree. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced at row level (descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included=false) and result level (freezeCp373Result stamps writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, synthetic_only=true, all dispatch flags false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, leak/permission_bypass=false). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE (manifest status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor; command-evidence implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout with all 24 commands exit 0); the Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is a derived candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid) gated behind human final approval, not an enterprise-trust claim. Claude stays a read-only reviewer (tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only=true, claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true). Hermes patterns are used only for descriptor-only evidence/receipt handling (receipt_shape descriptor_only_billing_p02_closeout_p03_foundation_no_write; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening readiness is clean: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1454-byte non-zero valid-JSON stdout), prompt 2445 bytes under the 25600 limit with no full diff/source, summary-only review correctly prohibited (Risk C + security/review deliverables), forbidden tools absent, and changed-file-scope correctly isolates unrelated dirty files (LDIP invalid-attempt, workbook docs, docx) from the active pack. Post-review artifacts (raw-output, receipt, claude-review-result) correctly do not pre-exist this run. One non-blocking P3: conditional no-leak guards in the CP373 descriptor validator target model-definition row keys absent from this slice's service/contract/API vocabulary, but unconditional per-row guards plus result-level attestations fully cover the boundary; this mirrors the already-closed CP365-CP372 packs. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: Conditional no-leak guards in CP373 descriptor validator are inert for this slice's row vocabulary
findings.details: validateBillingCoreCp373P02CloseoutP03FoundationDescriptor includes a defensive per-micro-phase block (approx. lines 1960-1986) that checks specific descriptor row keys (tenant_scope_field, state_transition_map, validation_helper, fixture_model, model_unit_test, invalid_reference_test, hermes_model_summary, claude_model_review_prompt) for their no-leak/no-write sub-flags. CP00-373 covers M09 Claude Review tail, M10 Closeout, and the M00-M06 API/contract micro-phases, whose row keys are service/contract/API vocabulary (e.g., state_transition_enforcement, service_entrypoint_contract, public_export_map, request_contract, error_code_taxonomy, permission_annotation, api_fixture, contract_test) and do not match any of those guarded keys, so each `if (rows.X && ...)` short-circuits and never fires. This is not a boundary gap: the unconditional per-row guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false) are asserted for every row at lines 1954-1957, and freezeCp373Result stamps result-level attestations (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, synthetic_only=true, exposes_*=false, leak_detected=false, permission_bypass_detected=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, all dispatch flags false). The pattern is identical to the already-closed CP365-CP372 packs.
findings.recommendation: No action required for CP00-373 closeout. Optionally, in a future hardening pass, make intent explicit (comment that these are defense-in-depth for shared model-definition row vocabularies) or add a positive assertion that model-definition descriptor keys are not unexpectedly present in service/contract/API slices.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_acknowledged: consistent with established CP365-CP372 cross-pack pattern; pack remains descriptor-only within no-write/no-real-data boundaries; does not affect pack or goal closeout
